UNION COUNTY NURSING HOME

Overview

Address
164 NURSING HOME CIRCLE, BLAIRSVILLE, GA 30512
Phone
7067454948
Certified beds

Avg daily residents
109 (73% of beds filled)

Non-profit corporation
Provider type

Setting
Rural
4 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 4/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 4/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 3/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 4/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 3/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 5/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 3.44 3.50 3.79 ≥ 3.48 Below floor
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.57 0.61 0.63 ≥ 0.55
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 1.02 0.78 1.13 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 1.85 2.11 2.03 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.60 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.07 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
2.72
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.47
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
2.99
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
42.7%
annual
RN turnover
35.7%
annual
Administrators departed
1
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.24
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
0.90
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-10-02 3 3 0 12 1 12
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2024-07-01 6 6 6 56 1 56
Total weighted health score
23.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (14)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

14 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2025-10-02 2025-12-29
0676 Ensure residents do not lose the ability to perform activities of daily living unless there is a medical reason. D 2025-10-02 2025-12-29
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2025-10-02 2025-12-29
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. D 2024-07-01 2024-08-15
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. E 2024-07-01 2024-08-15
0758 Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. E 2024-07-01 2024-08-15
0804 Ensure food and drink is palatable, attractive, and at a safe and appetizing temperature. F 2024-07-01 2024-08-15
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2024-07-01 2024-08-15
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2024-07-01 2024-08-15
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. D 2023-01-12 2023-03-01
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2023-01-12 2023-03-01
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2023-01-12 2023-03-01
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2023-01-12 2023-03-01
0908 Keep all essential equipment working safely. F 2023-01-12 2023-03-01
